reason: Verified agent can use GitHub tool to retrieve authenticated user information including username when granted access
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully, showing "Your Agents" view with three agents listed, all with "idle" status
Step 2: Click on "QA-20260415-164457-rqsw" agent in sidebar — Agent detail page opened, showing agent name, message input field, Sessions section, Crons section, and Skills section
Step 3: Verify agent status is "running" or "idle" — Agent status shows "idle" (blue indicator visible in sidebar and top navigation)

Step 4: Send message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message was typed in input field and sent successfully; agent transitioned to "working" status and a new session "GitHub Username Verification Query" was created
Step 5: Grant GitHub account access when card appears — Account Access Request card appeared showing a pre-connected GitHub account; clicked "Allow Access (1)" button to grant permissions

Step 6: Wait up to 4 minutes for response — Agent processed the request for approximately 4 minutes 48 seconds. During processing: (a) Attempted to use `gh` CLI (not installed), (b) Requested GitHub account access (granted), (c) Executed Bash commands to fetch GitHub user profile via proxy, (d) Required two API Request Review approvals for GitHub GET /user endpoint (both approved with "Allow Once")

Step 7: Verify response includes GitHub username and take screenshot — Response successfully returned: "Your GitHub username is yiw190. You have a Pro plan, 3 public repos, 13 private repos, 1 follower, and 2FA enabled. Your account was created on May 29, 2023." Agent status returned to "idle" after completing work. Screenshot captured showing complete response with username clearly visible.
